howItWorks.intro: This bonus tax calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Flat method: 22% federal withholding on bonuses under $1M
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: Why are bonuses taxed so heavily?
faq.answer: Bonuses are not taxed at a higher rate — they are withheld at a flat 22% federal rate (or 37% over $1M). Your actual tax rate depends on total annual income.

faq.question: Will I get some back at tax time?
faq.answer: If the flat 22% withholding exceeds your effective tax rate, yes — you will get a refund. If your marginal rate is higher than 22%, you may owe more.

faq.question: What is the aggregate method?
faq.answer: Your employer combines your bonus with regular pay for that period and withholds based on the combined amount. This often results in higher withholding.

faq.question: Does FICA apply to bonuses?
faq.answer: Yes. Social Security (6.2%) and Medicare (1.45%) apply to bonuses. Social Security has a wage cap of $168,600 in 2026.

faq.question: Is the Bonus Tax Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Bonus Tax Calculator?
sections.body: The Bonus Tax Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your bonus amount, method, state tax rate, you get instant results including federal tax, state tax, fica. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting federal tax right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your bonus amount and need to find the right federal tax. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
